sections.paragraph:
συνοδία, ας, ἡ a group of travelers, caravan (so Strabo 4, 6, 6; 11, 14, 4; Epict. 4, 1, 91; OGI 633, 1; 638, 7; 646, 6; Jos., Bell. 2, 587, Ant. 6, 243; loanw. in rabb.—2 Esdr 17:5, 64 συνοδία means ‘family’) Lk 2:44.—New Docs 2, 209;—DELG s.v. ὁδός. M-M.

Strabo Strabo , I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Epict Epict , various works, I–II a.d.—List 5

OGI OGI = Orientis Graeci Inscriptiones Selectae—List 3

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

loanw. loanw. = loanword

rabb. rabb. = rabbinical (literature etc.)

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 973
